key_points: Gopi was a kind and imaginative girl who received a magic brush
The magic brush had the power to make whatever she painted become real
Gopi used the brush to help poor villagers by painting useful things
A greedy Zamindar tried to force Gopi to paint treasures for him
Gopi used her creativity and courage to escape and continued helping others
examples: Gopi painted a bowl of khichdi which became real
She painted a plough to help a farmer work in the field
The Zamindar demanded that she paint a fortress and treasures
Gopi painted a river to stop the Zamindar and his soldiers
She painted a feast of sweets and tea to celebrate with the villagers
formulas_or_processes: Introduction → Gopi receives the magic brush from a mysterious man
Conflict → The greedy Zamindar demands riches from Gopi
Action → Gopi refuses and uses creativity to escape
Resolution → The Zamindar is defeated and Gopi continues helping villagers
exam_tips: Remember that the magic brush symbolizes creativity and imagination
Understand why Gopi refused to help the greedy Zamindar
Focus on how Gopi used clever thinking to escape danger
Know the moral: kindness and creativity can defeat greed
